headline: Meridian plc claims approximately £6 million against TechFlow for the allegedly defective and delayed implementation of a cloud-based retail inventory and EPOS platform under a Master Services Agreement dated 14 March 2024.
paragraphs: This is a UK commercial claim for breach of contract, negligence and misrepresentation arising from TechFlow's design, build and implementation of a cloud-based inventory management and electronic point-of-sale platform for a major multi-channel retailer. The platform went live on 18 November 2024 and, Meridian alleges, suffered severe outages and stock-synchronisation defects throughout the critical November and December 2024 peak trading period, causing both wasted contractual expenditure and substantial lost profit.

Meridian is a public limited company operating approximately 180 UK retail stores and an online sales channel; TechFlow is a retail software systems supplier that agreed under the MSA to design, configure and implement the platform for total charges of £2.4 million. Meridian claims £1.8 million in wasted expenditure and £4.2 million in lost profits, while TechFlow denies liability in its entirety, counterclaiming £600,000 in withheld milestone payments and relying on Meridian's own signed Change Order No. 3 (extending go-live to 18 November), a signed Acceptance Certificate dated 12 November 2024, Meridian's Programme Director's rejection of TechFlow's written recommendation to defer launch to January 2025, a third-party network failure as the cause of the most significant outage, and a contractual cap and exclusion of loss-of-profit liability under clause 14.

Of the 61 pleaded propositions, 22 are evidentially supported, 28 remain contested and 8 are undermined, producing a trial readiness of only 59%; the undermined propositions strike heavily at the Claimant's case, eroding its central contentions that the go-live date was fixed and time was of the essence (P0014–P0015), that no scope changes were requested (P0021), that no acceptance was given (P0034) and that TechFlow proceeded to go-live in the face of Meridian's warnings (P0023–P0025), all of which are contradicted by the Defendant's supported documentary evidence. Three propositions remain entirely without evidential support — the negligence cause of action (P0005), the misrepresentation cause of action (P0006) and the critical factual allegation that the platform was incapable of 10,000 concurrent transactions (P0018) — representing material gaps that must be plugged before trial. The EU legal materials supplied from the Cellar repository (relating to banana import licences, EU civil service non-contractual liability, competition fines, customs remission and indirect capital taxes) are entirely inapplicable to this purely domestic UK contract, negligence and misrepresentation dispute and should be disregarded.
